title: dianthus illyricus subsp. angustifolius
intro: Dianthus illyricus subsp. angustifolius, also known as narrow-leaved Illyrian pink or Illyrian pink, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. It is a perennial herb that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Dianthus illyricus subsp. angustifolius can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
